channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump attends the Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was huddling with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, taking in one of the most storied rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jamie Foxx reportedly injured in altercation at Beverly Hills restaurant
item.description: Foxx, who turned 57 on Friday, was dining with family at Mr. Chow when patrons at a nearby table hurled rude and vulgar insults to the "In Living Color" and "Ray" star, a witness told TMZ.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 ways to avoid a financial holiday hangover
item.description: The festivity of December is replaced all too quickly by the due dates of January, when the bills from holiday spending and travel arrive.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thousands of Santas and a few Grinches hit the streets for annual SantaCon bar crawls
item.description: Thousands of Kris Kringles and jolly Old St. Nicks are jamming streets, sidewalks and bars from Adelaide to Vancouver on Saturday for annual SantaCon charity pub crawls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Storms encase Iowa and eastern Nebraska in ice and generate rare tornado warning in San Francisco
item.description: A major ice storm created treacherous driving conditions across Iowa and eastern Nebraska this weekend and prompted temporary closures of Interstate 80 after numerous cars and trucks slid off the road.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, ABC 4

item.title: Here's what to know about the US push for stability in post-Assad Syria
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken has wrapped up perhaps his last Middle East as America’s top diplomat, with the aim of preventing Syria from spiraling out of control after the sudden ouster of President Bashar Assad. Blinken was one of several senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), who was hospitalized during a trip to Luxembourg, underwent hip surgery early Saturday, according to a spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Elizabeth Stuart North, beloved wife and mother, dead at 80
item.description: Elizabeth Stuart North, beloved wife of retired Lt. Col. Oliver North, is remembered as "bright, beautiful, bountiful and brave" by her four children in an obituary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Top diplomats from US, Arab League and Turkey discuss Syria's transition
item.description: Top diplomats from the United States, the Arab League and Turkey met in Jordan on Saturday to discuss how to assist Syria ’s transition after the fall of Bashar Assad's government a week ago. No Syrian representatives attended.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A look back at the most notable bankruptcies of 2024
item.description: This year saw its fair share of bankruptcies, with household names and century-old companies in the mix.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KREX 5

item.title: Obesity rate among US adults fell in 2023 for first time in over a decade: Study
item.description: The obesity rate among U.S. adults fell in 2023 for the first time in over a decade, according to a new study released on Friday. The study published in the journal JAMA Health Forum found that obesity nationwide has dropped from 46 percent in 2022 to 45.6 percent last year.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: New Jersey drones: State senator wants state of emergency
item.description: One politician in New Jersey is calling for a "limited" state of emergency after what he said is a lack of government transparency over the recent unexplained wave of drone sightings.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Texas father set house on fire with 3 children inside: police
item.description: A Texas father is facing charges over allegations that he intentionally set his home on fire with his three children inside, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Detroit area's many Syrians are celebrating Assad's overthrow and planning long-delayed visits
item.description: DEARBORN, Mich. (AP) — Nizam Abazid is gleefully planning his first trip in decades to Syria, where he grew up. Rama Alhoussaini was only 6 years old when her family moved to the U.S., but she's excited about the prospect of introducing her three kids to relatives they've never met in person.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Winter weather safety: Why bulky coats could be dangerous for children in car seats
item.description: Here are some winter weather tips for drivers, including some on how to make sure children stay safe and warm while in their car seats.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, Fox 5

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting suspect fled New York via train, not by bus as originally thought: reports
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, the man accused of fatally shooting UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, is believed to have taken a train out of Manhattan, not a bus as originally thought.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Manchin: Biden 'went completely insane on me' over American Rescue Plan opposition
item.description: “I was going to kill the whole bill. Then the president went completely insane on me,” Manchin said in an interview with news outlet Semafor that was published on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court will take up a challenge related to California's tough vehicle emissions standards
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a business-backed appeal that could make it easier to challenge federal regulations, acting in a dispute related to California's nation-leading standards for vehicle emissions.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
